items.title: CMS issues guidance on upcoming limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS released a State Health Official letter advising states that beginning 2026-10-01 they must limit federal financial participation (FFP) for Medicaid and CHIP to eligible categories of noncitizens under the new statutory limits. CMS noted exceptions including continued federal matching funds for emergency medical conditions and that the change does not apply to states’ options to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: Medicaid.gov posts SHO #26-001 detailing implementation steps for section 71109 noncitizen FFP limits
items.summary: The SHO letter states the implementation date is 2026-10-01 and describes operational impacts for applicants and existing enrollees on that date, including eligibility systems and proper FFP claiming for Medicaid and CHIP. It also notes CHIP-specific implications, including that beginning 2026-10-01 FFP will no longer be available for some CHIP beneficiaries in a continuous eligibility period under specified statutory provisions.

items.title: HUD PIH Notice 2026-06 rescinds multiple COVID-era notices affecting Housing Choice Vouchers and public housing administration
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-06 (issued 2026-03-16) rescinds a long list of COVID-19-related PIH notices (including CARES Act HCV admin fee and HAP supplemental funding guidance) and revises some remaining guidance. HUD said this is because pandemic-related guidance is no longer necessary or has become obsolete, while emphasizing PHAs must still comply with federal civil-rights language-access obligations.

items.title: HUD publishes PIH Notices 2026-04 and 2026-05 rolling back certain flexibilities for vouchers and homelessness-related admissions
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-04 rescinds Notice PIH 2024-17, which had allowed streamlined verification flexibilities (e.g., temporary self-certification for certain documents) to expedite admissions for people experiencing homelessness. HUD Notice PIH 2026-05 revises the Emergency Housing Voucher and Stability Voucher programs by rescinding flexibility that allowed PHAs to delay collecting Social Security and citizenship/eligible immigration documentation within 180 days of admission.

items.title: USDA approves Kansas SNAP demonstration to restrict purchases of candy and soft drinks (effective 2027-02-15)
items.summary: USDA FNS approved Kansas’ request to amend the SNAP definition of eligible food to exclude candy and soft drinks. The approval is for two years and is effective 2027-02-15.

items.title: USDA approves Ohio SNAP demonstration to restrict purchases of sugar-sweetened beverages (effective 2026-10-01)
items.summary: USDA FNS approved Ohio’s request to amend the SNAP definition of eligible food to exclude sugar-sweetened beverages. The approval is for two years and is effective 2026-10-01.

items.title: USDA reminds WIC agencies: by April 2026, authorize at least one non-fresh form of fruits/vegetables for the cash-value benefit
items.summary: USDA FNS said recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to allow participants to buy at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026. FNS reported that as of November 2025, 95% of state agencies were already in compliance.

items.title: About $3.6B in LIHEAP heating assistance released to states and tribes after delay
items.summary: An AP report published by PBS NewsHour said about $3.6 billion in postponed LIHEAP funding was released to states and tribes, citing the National Energy Assistance Directors Association (NEADA). The report notes the delay was tied to a federal government shutdown that ended 2025-11-12.

items.title: SSA posts 2026 SSI federal payment amounts after 2.8% COLA
items.summary: SSA’s COLA page shows a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment effective January 2026. The maximum federal SSI monthly payment amounts for 2026 are $994 for an eligible individual, $1,491 for an eligible couple, and $498 for an essential person.
